title: Rename embed multimethod to text-embedding
description: Rename the embed multimethod to text-embedding for clarity. Update all call sites accordingly.

title: TUI auto-reconnect when WebSocket disconnects
description:
The TUI client should automatically reconnect when the WebSocket connection is lost.

## Use Case
During development, restarting the server disconnects the TUI. Currently requires manual restart of the client.

## Desired Behavior
1. Detect WebSocket disconnection
2. Update UI to show "Disconnected - Reconnecting..." status
3. Attempt reconnection with exponential backoff:
   - 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 30s
4. On successful reconnect:
   - Update UI to show "Connected"
   - Re-authenticate if needed (use saved JWT)
   - Resume normal operation
5. After N failed attempts, show "Connection failed - press R to retry"

## UI Status Indicator
Header should show connection status:
- `[Connected]` - normal operation (green)
- `[Disconnected - Reconnecting...]` - attempting reconnect (yellow)
- `[Disconnected - Press R to retry]` - gave up, manual retry (red)

## Implementation
1. Add connection state to TUI model (:connected, :reconnecting, :disconnected)
2. Add WebSocket close handler that triggers reconnect loop
3. Add reconnect logic with exponential backoff
4. Update view to show connection status in header
5. Add 'R' keybind to manually trigger reconnect

title: TUI silently ignores WebSocket errors
description:
When WebSocket requests fail (e.g., LLM not running), TUI shows no error to user.

## Root Cause
`update.clj` doesn't handle `:ws-error` message type - falls through to default which ignores it.

## Infrastructure Already Exists
- `ws.clj:47-52` - Produces `:ws-error` messages
- `core.clj:46` - `set-error` function
- `view.clj:158` - `render-error` function

## Fix
Add handler in `update.clj`:

```clojure
(defn- handle-ws-error
  [state msg]
  [(core/set-error state (:message msg)) nil])

;; In update-fn case statement:
:ws-error (handle-ws-error state msg)
```

title: Create config entity for tunable settings
description:
Create a config entity to store tunable application settings.

## Schema
```clojure
{:kind  (s/kind :config)
 :id    {:type :long}
 :key   {:type :keyword}
 :value {:type :string}}
```

## Migration
```sql
CREATE TABLE config (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  key VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  value TEXT
);
```

## Initial Config Keys
| Key | Default | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| :dedupe-enabled | true | Master switch for deduplication |
| :dedupe-embedding-threshold | 0.85 | Min similarity for LLM check |
| :dedupe-max-candidates | 5 | Max candidates to check with LLM |
| :dedupe-high-seen-threshold | 3 | seen-count above this = already known |

## API
```clojure
(config/get :dedupe-enabled)           ; => true
(config/set! :dedupe-enabled false)    ; update value
```

title: Create conversation entity and schema
description:
Store conversations between users and Isaac.

## Schema: Conversation
```clojure
{:kind         (s/kind :conversation)
 :id           {:type :long}
 :user-id      {:type :long}           ; who is chatting
 :started-at   {:type :instant}
 :updated-at   {:type :instant}
 :status       {:type :keyword}}       ; :active, :closed
```

## Schema: Message
```clojure
{:kind            (s/kind :message)
 :id              {:type :long}
 :conversation-id {:type :long}
 :role            {:type :keyword}     ; :user, :isaac
 :content         {:type :string}
 :created-at      {:type :instant}
 :thought-ids     {:type [:long]}}     ; thoughts born from this message
```

## Features
- Multiple conversations can be active simultaneously
- Messages reference thoughts they generated
- Conversations belong to users

title: Implement conversation chat handler
description:
Handle chat messages from users and generate Isaac responses.

## Flow
1. User sends message
2. Retrieve conversation history (last N messages)
3. Retrieve relevant thoughts via embedding search
4. Build prompt with context + conversation history
5. Call LLM for response
6. Parse response for new thoughts (INSIGHT, QUESTION, etc.)
7. Store new thoughts with reference to message
8. Store Isaac's response message
9. Return response to user

## API
```clojure
(defn chat! [conversation-id user-message]
  ;; Returns {:response "..." :thoughts [...]}
  )
```

## Prompt Structure
```
[Three Laws]
[Relevant Thoughts]
[Conversation History]
[User Message]
```

## Depends on
- Conversation/message schema

title: Grok LLM integration
description:
Implement Grok (xAI) as an LLM provider using the existing multimethod pattern.

## Config
```clojure
{:llm {:impl  :grok
       :url   "https://api.x.ai/v1"
       :model "grok-3-latest"}}
```

## Implementation

### llm/grok.clj (new file)
- Implement `llm/chat :grok` method
- Implement `llm/chat-with-tools :grok` method
- Use `(secret/get-secret "GROK_API_KEY")` for auth
- OpenAI-compatible API format

### API Details
- Endpoint: `https://api.x.ai/v1/chat/completions`
- Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>`
- Request/response format matches OpenAI spec

## Dependencies
- Requires secret source abstraction (isaac-6pk)

title: Terminal client for Isaac using charm.clj
description:
Build a terminal UI client in Clojure using charm.clj to interact with Isaac.

## Why charm.clj?
- Pure Clojure - full code reuse with Isaac server
- Bubble Tea-inspired (Elm Architecture: Model-Update-View)
- Runs on JVM, native-image, or Babashka
- https://codeberg.org/timokramer/charm.clj

## Features
- Connect to Isaac via WebSocket
- View Isaac's current thinking status
- List and manage goals
- View recent thoughts and insights
- See shares (things Isaac wants to tell you)
- Chat with Isaac
- Real-time updates as Isaac thinks

## UI Layout
```
┌─ Isaac ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Status: Thinking about "Learn Clojure macros"       │
├─ Goals ─────────────────┬─ Recent Thoughts ──────────┤
│ [active] Learn macros   │ I think macros allow...    │
│ [active] Help Micah     │ Perhaps I should start...  │
│ [done] Setup project    │ An insight: code is data   │
├─────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┤
│ > _                                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Commands
- `/goals` - List goals
- `/add <goal>` - Add a goal
- `/thoughts` - Recent thoughts
- `/shares` - View shares
- Free text → chat with Isaac

## Code Reuse Opportunities
- Schema definitions (thought, friend, goal)
- WebSocket message types
- Domain logic
- Utility functions

## Depends on
- WebSocket API (isaac-9pi)

title: Move think service start fn into think namespace
description:
Move the `-start-think` function and `think-service` definition from `main.clj` into the `think` namespace.

## Current State
`mdm.isaac.main` contains:
```clojure
(defn -start-think [app]
  (think/start-think app ollama/chat {:delay-ms ...}))
(def think-service (app/service 'mdm.isaac.main/-start-think 'mdm.isaac.think/stop-think))
```

## Goal
Move this logic into `mdm.isaac.think` so the think namespace is self-contained.

## Implementation
1. Move `-start-think` to `think.clj`
2. Define `think-service` in `think.clj`
3. Update `main.clj` to reference `think/service`

title: Add DJL/ONNX embedding provider for in-process embeddings
description:
Implement :onnx or :djl provider in mdm.isaac.embedding namespace for in-process embeddings without external service dependency.

Prerequisites:
- Install ARM64 JVM (e.g., brew install --cask temurin or ARM64 Corretto)
- Verify with: java -XshowSettings:properties -version 2>&1 | grep os.arch (should show aarch64)

Implementation:
- Add DJL dependencies to deps.edn (ai.djl.huggingface/tokenizers, ai.djl.onnxruntime/onnxruntime-engine)
- Use sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model (384 dimensions)
- Use TextEmbeddingTranslatorFactory for tokenization and inference

Benefits:
- No Ollama service required
- No HTTP overhead
- Runs entirely in JVM process

Reference: Previous attempt failed due to x86_64 JVM on Apple Silicon - DJL native libs require ARM64 JVM on macOS.

title: Migrate to AWS SDK v2 credentials
description:
Use AWS SDK v2 credentials consistently to remove the need for both v1 and v2 in deps.edn.

## Current State
`aws.clj` uses AWS SDK v1 credentials:
```clojure
(import com.amazonaws.auth.AWSStaticCredentialsProvider)
(import com.amazonaws.auth.BasicAWSCredentials)
(import com.amazonaws.auth.InstanceProfileCredentialsProvider)
```

## Goal
Migrate to AWS SDK v2:
```clojure
(import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.StaticCredentialsProvider)
(import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.AwsBasicCredentials)
(import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.InstanceProfileCredentialsProvider)
```

## Benefits
- Remove AWS SDK v1 from deps.edn
- Consistent API usage
- Smaller dependency footprint

title: Thinking loop
description:
Isaac's continuous thinking loop - the core of his autonomous behavior.

## Behavior
1. Query for active goals
2. Pick goal by priority or interest
3. Retrieve relevant context (past thoughts via embedding search)
4. Call LLM to reason about the goal
5. Store resulting thoughts (insights, questions, sub-goals)
6. If no conclusion after N iterations, move to another goal
7. Rate-limit to avoid excessive LLM calls

## Implementation
1. Add thinking loop to `main.clj` (or new `mdm.isaac.think` namespace)
2. Goal selection logic
3. Context retrieval using embedding search
4. LLM prompt for reasoning
5. Parse and store LLM output as typed thoughts
6. Loop timing and goal-switching logic

## Depends on
- Goal management (needs goals to think about)
- Embedding search (isaac-far - for context retrieval)

## Three Laws
The thinking loop should be guided by Asimov's Three Laws:
1. Do no harm
2. Obey friends (unless it conflicts with #1)
3. Self-preserve (unless it conflicts with #1 or #2)

title: Refactor multimethod dispatch to use config-based db impl
description: Replace hardcoded impl constant with config-based dispatch. The save multimethod should dispatch off of (-> config/active :db :impl) instead of a hardcoded impl var. Once done, delete the impl constant.

title: Add chat mode to TUI
description:
Enable free-form chat with Isaac in the TUI.

## Current
- Commands like /goals, /thoughts, /add
- Free text goes to 'chat' but minimal implementation

## New Behavior
- Free text enters conversation mode
- Isaac responds conversationally
- Conversation history shown in main panel
- Can still use / commands

## UI Changes
```
┌─ Isaac ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Status: Thinking about "Learn Clojure"              │
├─ Conversation ───────────────────────────────────────┤
│ You: How are you doing today?                        │
│                                                      │
│ Isaac: I'm doing well! Currently thinking about      │
│ Clojure macros. I had an insight that macros are     │
│ essentially code that writes code...                 │
│                                                      │
│ You: Tell me more about that insight.                │
│                                                      │
│ Isaac: ...                                           │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ > _                                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Depends on
- Conversation chat handler
- WebSocket support for streaming (nice to have)

title: Password visible in plain text during TUI login
description: When running the TUI client and prompted for login credentials, the password is displayed in plain text as the user types. Should mask the password with asterisks or hide it completely using System/console .readPassword like the user creation CLI does.

title: Create tool system for Isaac
description:
Allow Isaac to use tools during conversation, similar to Claude.

## Architecture
```clojure
(defprotocol Tool
  (tool-name [this])
  (tool-description [this])
  (execute [this params]))
```

## Initial Tools
| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| create-goal | Create a new goal |
| list-goals | View active goals |
| update-goal | Change goal status/priority |
| search-thoughts | Find relevant thoughts |
| create-thought | Store a new thought |

## Future Tools
- read-file (with approval)
- edit-file (with approval)
- run-command (with approval)
- reconfigure-self (with approval)

## LLM Integration
Include tool descriptions in prompt. Parse tool calls from response.

## Approval Flow
Some tools require user approval before execution:
1. Isaac proposes tool use
2. User sees: "Isaac wants to edit config.edn. Allow? [y/n]"
3. User approves/denies
4. Tool executes or is cancelled

title: Secret source abstraction with :env and :aws impls
description:
Create a multimethod-based secret retrieval system that dispatches on `:secret-source :impl`.

## API
```clojure
(get-secret "GROK_API_KEY")  ; string in, string out
```

## Implementation

### secret.clj (new file)
- Multimethod `get-secret` dispatching on `(-> config/active :secret-source :impl)`
- `:env` impl: `(System/getenv name)`
- `:default` impl: throw ex-info

### aws.clj (extend)
- `:aws` impl: `(get-secret (str prefix name) region)`
- Uses existing `get-secret` function

### config.clj (update)
- Development: `{:secret-source {:impl :env}}`
- Staging/Production: `{:secret-source {:impl :aws :region "us-west-2" :prefix "isaac/"}}`

## Not in scope
- JWT secret migration
- GCP implementation

title: Deduplicate thoughts using embedding similarity
description:
Before storing a new thought, check if a similar thought already exists.

## Problem
The thinking loop may generate duplicate or near-duplicate thoughts, causing:
- Database bloat
- Redundant search results
- Wasted processing

## Solution
Use embedding similarity to detect duplicates before saving.

```clojure
(defn save-if-unique! [thought threshold]
  (let [embedding (text-embedding (:content thought))
        similar (find-similar embedding 5)]
    (if (any-above-threshold? similar threshold)
      (log/debug "Skipping duplicate thought")
      (save! (assoc thought :embedding embedding)))))
```

## Implementation
1. Define similarity threshold (e.g., 0.95 cosine similarity)
2. Before saving, query for similar thoughts via `find-similar`
3. If any result exceeds threshold, skip or merge
4. Log when duplicates are detected (for tuning threshold)

## Depends on
- Embedding search (isaac-far) - need `find-similar` to check for duplicates

## Considerations
- Threshold tuning: too high = duplicates slip through, too low = legitimate thoughts blocked
- Performance: adds a search query per thought creation
- Could scope to thought type (e.g., only dedupe :insight, not :question)

title: Sharing system
description:
Enable Isaac to share thoughts with friends.

## Behavior
- When Isaac has something to share, create thought with :type :share
- Print to stdout when share is created (so Micah sees it if watching)
- Query interface to review pending/unread shares
- Mark shares as read/acknowledged

## Implementation
1. Share creation (already covered by thought types)
2. Print hook when :share thought is created
3. Add :read flag or :shared-at timestamp
4. Query: `(unread-shares)` or `(shares-for-friend friend-id)`
5. Mark as read: `(acknowledge-share thought-id)`

## Depends on
- Friend schema (shares are for friends)
- Thinking loop (generates shares)

title: Move Ollama implementation to mdm.isaac.embedding.ollama
description: Extract the Ollama embedding implementation (ollama-url, ollama-model, embed :ollama method) to its own namespace mdm.isaac.embedding.ollama.

title: WebSocket API for Isaac server
description:
Expose Isaac's functionality via WebSocket API for client connections.

## Endpoints / Messages

### Client → Server
- `goals:list` - Get all goals
- `goals:add` - Add a new goal
- `goals:update` - Update goal status/priority
- `thoughts:recent` - Get recent thoughts
- `thoughts:search` - Search thoughts by embedding
- `shares:unread` - Get unread shares
- `shares:ack` - Acknowledge a share
- `chat:send` - Send a message to Isaac
- `subscribe:thoughts` - Subscribe to thought stream

### Server → Client
- `thought:new` - New thought created (for subscribers)
- `share:new` - Isaac has something to share
- `chat:response` - Response to chat message
- `status:update` - Isaac's current state (thinking about X)

## Implementation
1. Add WebSocket handler namespace `mdm.isaac.ws`
2. Use c3kit.wire.websocket for connection management
3. JSON message format for cross-language compatibility
4. Authentication (simple token for now)

## Tech
- c3kit.wire.websocket (already a dependency)
- JSON encoding for messages

title: Move main into server namespace
description:
Reorganize main entry point to support separate server and CLI.

## Changes
1. Move `mdm.isaac.main` → `mdm.isaac.server.main`
2. Update deps.edn alias from `run` to `server`
3. Update the main function reference in the alias

## Rationale
With the addition of a TUI client (charm.clj), we need separate entry points:
- `server` - runs Isaac's thinking loop and WebSocket API
- `cli` - runs the terminal interface (future)

## Implementation
1. Create `src/mdm/isaac/server/main.clj`
2. Move contents from `src/mdm/isaac/main.clj`
3. Update namespace references
4. Update deps.edn:
   ```clojure
   :server {:main-opts ["-m" "mdm.isaac.server.main"]}
   ```
5. Delete old `src/mdm/isaac/main.clj`
6. Update any specs that reference the old namespace

title: Add seen-count field to thought schema
description:
Track how many times a thought has been seen (for deduplication).

## Schema Change
Add to thought:
```clojure
:seen-count {:type :long}  ; default 1
```

## Migration
```sql
ALTER TABLE thought ADD COLUMN seen_count INTEGER DEFAULT 1;
```

## Purpose
When a duplicate thought is detected, increment seen-count on the original instead of saving a new record. High seen-count thoughts can be sent to the LLM as "already known" to prevent future duplicates.

title: Move domain modules to .core namespaces
description:
Reorganize domain modules to use a .core namespace pattern for consistency.

## Changes
- `mdm.isaac.goal` → `mdm.isaac.goal.core`
- `mdm.isaac.thought` → `mdm.isaac.thought.core`
- `mdm.isaac.share` → `mdm.isaac.share.core`

## Rationale
Follows the existing pattern (e.g., `embedding.core`, `thought.core`) where the main logic lives in a .core namespace, allowing room for related namespaces like .schema, .db, etc.

## Implementation
1. Create new .core namespaces
2. Move functions from parent namespace
3. Update all references
4. Keep parent namespace as facade (optional) or delete

title: Tool framework with tune-config
description: Create tool abstraction layer with protocol, registry, and permissions. Implement tune-config as first internal tool. Files: tool/schema.cljc, tool/core.clj, tool/internal/config.clj

title: Refactor commands to follow OCP
description:
Refactor the command handling in `client/update.clj` to follow the Open-Closed Principle.

## Problem
The current `cond` for command handling violates OCP - adding new commands requires modifying existing code.

## Solution
Create a command abstraction (deftype or protocol) that allows commands to be registered without modifying the dispatch logic.

## Locations
- `src/mdm/isaac/client/update.clj:11` - command cond
- `src/mdm/isaac/client/update.clj:73` - KeyCommands should also be deftypes

## Implementation
1. Define a Command protocol with `execute` and `name` methods
2. Create deftype for each command
3. Store commands in a registry (list or map)
4. Replace cond with registry lookup

title: Create embedding abstraction with multimethod; implement for Ollama and DJL
description:
Create an abstraction for generating embeddings using Clojure multimethods in an embedding namespace.

Requirements:
- Define multimethod in embedding namespace (e.g., `embed` dispatching on provider type)
- Implement Ollama backend (existing functionality)
- Implement DJL backend (new - uses Deep Java Library for in-process embeddings)
- DJL should use sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 or similar model

Benefits of DJL:
- No separate service needed (in-process)
- No HTTP overhead
- Just JVM dependencies
close_reason: Created embedding abstraction with multimethod. Ollama provider implemented. DJL provider could not be implemented due to platform incompatibility (JVM running as x86_64 on Apple Silicon - DJL native libraries don't support this configuration). A future issue should be created to revisit DJL when platform support improves or when running on a compatible platform.

title: Tag slow tests and update spec alias to exclude them
description: Tag postgres/integration tests as :slow and update the spec alias in deps.edn to exclude slow tests by default for faster feedback during development.

title: Delete duplicate schema files
description:
Remove duplicate schema files that already exist elsewhere.

## Files to Delete
- `src/mdm/isaac/schema/full.clj` - already exists as `mdm.isaac.schema`
- `src/mdm/isaac/schema/thought.clj` - already exists as `mdm.isaac.thought.schema`

## Implementation
1. Verify the canonical locations have the correct content
2. Update any references to the old locations
3. Delete the duplicate files

title: Update migration filenames to include hours and minutes
description:
Migration filenames should include hours and minutes (not just date) to preserve creation order.

## Problem
Current migration format: `YYYYMMDD_name.clj`
If multiple migrations are created on the same day, their order is ambiguous.

## Solution
New format: `YYYYMMDD_HHMM_name.clj`

## Implementation
1. Rename existing migrations to include time
2. Update any migration tooling/conventions to use new format
3. Document the naming convention

title: Integrate tools into think loop
description: Modify think.clj to include tool descriptions in prompts, parse tool calls from LLM response, execute tools, and feed results back into context.

title: Add ability to search for thoughts based on embeddings
description:
Find top N thoughts matching a given embedding vector.

Example: Find top 10 thoughts matching the embedding for "Transformers"

## Implementation

1. Add `find-similar` multimethod to `thought/core.clj`
   - Signature: `(find-similar embedding limit)` → top N thoughts by similarity

2. Implement `:postgres` backend in `thought/pg.clj`
   - Use pgvector cosine distance: `ORDER BY embedding <=> ?::vector LIMIT ?`

3. Implement `:memory` backend in `thought/memory.clj`
   - Cosine similarity calculation for testing

4. Re-export `find-similar` in `thought.clj` public API

5. Add tests for both backends

title: Use c3kit.bucket for all database interactions
description:
Refactor database code to use c3kit.bucket API instead of raw next.jdbc.

## Background
c3kit.bucket 2.9.0 now supports pgvector queries. Currently `thought/pg.clj` uses raw `next.jdbc` for all operations.

## Current State
`src/mdm/isaac/thought/pg.clj` uses:
- `jdbc/execute!` for raw SQL
- `jdbc/execute-one!` for single results
- Manual result mapping via `result->thought`
- Raw SQL for vector operations: `ORDER BY embedding <=> ?::vector`

## Goal
Replace with bucket's API:
- `db/tx` for saves/updates
- `db/find` for queries
- `db/delete` for deletions
- Bucket's pgvector support for similarity search

## Implementation
1. Update `thought/pg.clj` to use `c3kit.bucket.api`
2. Replace manual SQL with bucket operations
3. Use bucket's vector query support for `find-similar`
4. Remove `next.jdbc` dependency from this namespace
5. Keep raw JDBC only in migrations (appropriate there)

## Benefits
- Consistent API across codebase
- Schema validation via bucket
- Simpler code (no manual result mapping)
- Leverage bucket's connection pooling and transaction management

title: Add :type field to thought schema
description:
Add a :type field to the thought schema to categorize thoughts.

## Types
- :thought - general thoughts
- :goal - things Isaac wants to achieve
- :insight - conclusions or realizations
- :question - things Isaac is wondering about
- :share - things Isaac wants to tell friends

## Implementation
1. Update thought schema in `src/mdm/isaac/schema/thought.clj`
2. Create migration to add `type` column
3. Update save functions to handle type
4. Add query helpers to find thoughts by type

## Foundation
This is the foundation for goal-driven thinking.

title: Include high seen-count thoughts in thinking prompt
description:
Send frequently-seen thoughts to LLM as "already known" to prevent duplicates.

## Change to build-prompt
```clojure
(defn build-prompt [goal context]
  (let [high-seen (find-high-seen-thoughts)]
    (str ...
         "\n\n## Already Known (don't repeat these)\n"
         (->> high-seen
              (map :content)
              (str/join "\n- ")))))
```

## Query
```clojure
(defn find-high-seen-thoughts []
  (let [threshold (config/get :dedupe-high-seen-threshold 3)]
    (db/find :thought 
             :where [> :seen-count threshold]
             :take 10)))
```

## Purpose
Proactively prevent the LLM from generating thoughts it has already generated multiple times.

## Depends on
- seen-count field on thought
- config entity for threshold

title: Create with-config macro in spec-helper
description:
Create a macro `with-config` in mdm.isaac.spec-helper that allows temporary config overrides in tests.

**Desired usage:**
```clojure
(with-config {:embedding {:impl :mock}})
```

**Context:**
Currently tests use `redefs-around` to override `config/active`, which is verbose. A dedicated macro would be cleaner and could be used consistently across all specs that need config overrides.

**Location:**
- Add macro to `mdm.isaac.spec-helper` namespace
- Update existing specs that use `redefs-around [config/active ...]` to use the new macro
close_reason: Created with-config macro in spec-helper, updated djl_spec, core_spec, and ollama_spec to use it

title: Create user creation CLI tool
description:
Create an executable main that prompts for user credentials and stores them in the database.

## Implementation
1. Create `src/mdm/isaac/user/create.clj` with a `-main` function
2. Add deps.edn alias:
   ```clojure
   :create-user {:main-opts ["-m" "mdm.isaac.user.create"]}
   ```

## Behavior
```
$ clj -M:create-user
Email: micah@example.com
Password: ********
User created successfully!
```

## Features
- Prompt for email (validate format)
- Prompt for password (hide input if possible)
- Hash password before storing (use bcrypt or similar)
- Store user record in database
- Handle duplicate email error gracefully

## Dependencies
- Requires user table migration (isaac-vt0)
- May need password hashing library in deps.edn

title: Consolidate AWS credentials code
description:
Consolidate duplicate AWS credentials code between `email.clj` and `aws.clj`.

## Problem
`email.clj` has its own `make-credentials-provider` function that duplicates logic in `aws.clj`.

## Implementation
1. Create a single credentials provider function in `aws.clj`
2. Update `email.clj` to use the shared function
3. Remove duplicate code

title: Link thoughts to conversation source
description:
When a thought is born from a conversation, link it back to the source message.

## Schema Change
Add to thought:
```clojure
:source-message-id {:type :long}  ; optional, message that spawned this thought
```

## Bidirectional
- Message has :thought-ids (thoughts it created)
- Thought has :source-message-id (message it came from)

## Use Case
When reviewing a thought, can see the conversation context that created it.
When in a conversation, can see thoughts that were generated.

title: Goal management
description:
Enable Isaac to manage goals as a special type of thought.

## Features
1. Create goals (thoughts with :type :goal)
2. Query goals by priority/status
3. Mark goals as active/resolved/abandoned
4. Associate goals with friends (who gave the goal, who benefits)

## Implementation
1. Add :priority field to thought schema (or goal-specific fields)
2. Add :status field (:active, :resolved, :abandoned)
3. Create goal-specific query functions
4. Namespace: `mdm.isaac.goal`

## Depends on
- Thought types (needs :type field)

title: Add migration to set thought embedding length to 384 for DJL
description:
Change the embedding vector dimension from 768 to 384 to match DJL's output.

## Background
- DJL (sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2) produces 384-dimensional embeddings
- Ollama (embeddinggemma) produces 768-dimensional embeddings
- Current schema uses vector(768), which doesn't match DJL

## Implementation
1. Create migration to alter the embedding column:
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE thought 
   ALTER COLUMN embedding TYPE vector(384)
   ```

2. Note: This will truncate/fail if existing embeddings have 768 dimensions
   - May need to clear existing data or re-embed with DJL
   - Or make dimension configurable based on active embedding provider

## Considerations
- If switching between providers, embeddings won't be compatible anyway
- Could add a config check to validate dimension matches provider
- Migration filename should include hours/minutes per isaac-ezu

title: Implement thought deduplication
description:
Implement multi-step deduplication before saving thoughts.

## Deduplication Flow

1. **Exact string match** (free)
   - Lowercase compare against existing thoughts
   - If match: increment seen-count, skip save

2. **Embedding similarity filter** (cheap)
   - Find candidates above `:dedupe-embedding-threshold`
   - Limit to `:dedupe-max-candidates`

3. **LLM semantic check** (expensive)
   - For each candidate, ask LLM if semantically equivalent
   - Prompt: "Are these two thoughts semantically equivalent? Answer YES or NO."
   - If YES: increment seen-count on original, skip save

4. **Save if unique**
   - No duplicates found → save with seen-count = 1

## Implementation
```clojure
(defn save-if-unique! [thought llm-fn]
  (let [content (str/lower-case (:content thought))
        embedding (:embedding thought)]
    (cond
      ;; Exact match
      (find-exact-match content)
      (increment-seen-count! %)

      ;; Semantic match via LLM
      (find-semantic-match embedding llm-fn)
      (increment-seen-count! %)

      ;; Unique - save it
      :else
      (db/tx (assoc thought :seen-count 1)))))
```

## Depends on
- seen-count field on thought
- config entity for thresholds

title: Add dimensions multimethod to embedding providers
description:
Add a multimethod to return embedding vector dimensions for each provider.

## Problem
Magic number dimensions are hardcoded (DJL=384, Ollama=768).
This causes potential mismatches between schema and actual embeddings.

## Implementation
1. Add `dimensions` multimethod to `embedding/core.clj`
2. Implement for `:djl` → returns 384
3. Implement for `:ollama` → returns 768
4. Replace magic numbers throughout codebase with `(embedding/dimensions)`

## Origin
Converted from TODO in `src/mdm/isaac/embedding/core.clj:4`

title: MCP client for external tools
description: HTTP-based MCP client using Streamable HTTP transport. Connect to external MCP servers like Brave Search. Files: tool/mcp/client.clj, tool/mcp/protocol.clj

title: Create thinking loop service using c3kit.apron.app/service
description:
Create a service for the thinking loop that integrates with c3kit's app lifecycle.

## Background
Isaac's thinking loop should run continuously as a managed service, starting with the app and stopping gracefully on shutdown.

## Implementation
1. Create `mdm.isaac.think` namespace with:
   - `start-think` function - initializes and starts the thinking loop
   - `stop-think` function - gracefully stops the loop
   - The loop itself (query goals, reason, store thoughts)

2. Define service in `main.clj`:
```clojure
(def think-service 
  (app/service 'mdm.isaac.think/start-think 
               'mdm.isaac.think/stop-think))
```

3. Add to `all-services`:
```clojure
(def all-services [env db/service think-service])
```

## Service Behavior
- Start: Begin thinking loop in background thread
- Stop: Signal loop to stop, wait for current thought to complete
- Rate-limited to avoid excessive LLM calls

## Depends on
- Thinking loop implementation (isaac-38r)

title: TUI client auto-login with saved JWT token
description:
The TUI client should save the JWT token locally and use it for automatic authentication.

## Behavior
1. On successful login, save JWT token to `~/.isaac/jwt`
2. On startup, check for existing token at `~/.isaac/jwt`
3. If token exists and is valid, use it to authenticate automatically
4. If token is expired or invalid, prompt for login credentials
5. On logout, delete the token file

## Implementation
1. Create `~/.isaac/` directory if it doesn't exist
2. Save token as plain text (or consider encryption)
3. Check token expiration before using (decode JWT to check `exp` claim)
4. Handle token refresh if supported by server

## Security Considerations
- Set file permissions to 600 (owner read/write only)
- Consider encrypting token at rest
- Clear token on explicit logout

## File Structure
```
~/.isaac/
  jwt           # JWT token file
  config.edn    # (future) user preferences
```

title: TUI displays chat messages twice
description:
User messages appear twice in the conversation panel.

## Root Cause
Message is added twice by the client:

1. `command.clj:121-124` - `enter-handler` adds message locally (optimistic update)
2. `main.clj:118-122` - `send-command!` puts `:chat/send-user` on the channel

## Fix
Remove the duplicate in `main.clj:118-122`. The `enter-handler` approach is cleaner since it's the optimistic update pattern.

```clojure
;; DELETE this block in main.clj
(when (= :chat (:action parsed))
  (when-let [ch @msg-chan]
    (put! ch {:type :ws-message
              :action :chat/send-user
              :payload {:content (:text parsed)}})))
```

title: Abstract UI to prevent test output
description:
Tests are printing output like "[Isaac wants to share]: First share" which clutters test results.

## Problem
Code uses `println` directly, which outputs during tests.

## Solution
Abstract the UI so tests can use a mock implementation.

### 1. Create UI protocol
```clojure
(defprotocol UI
  (inform [this message])
  (warn [this message])
  (error [this message]))
```

### 2. Create implementations
```clojure
;; Production - prints to console
(defrecord ConsoleUI []
  UI
  (inform [_ msg] (println msg))
  (warn [_ msg] (println "[WARN]" msg))
  (error [_ msg] (println "[ERROR]" msg)))

;; Testing - stores messages for inspection
(defrecord MockUI [messages]
  UI
  (inform [_ msg] (swap! messages conj {:type :info :msg msg}))
  (warn [_ msg] (swap! messages conj {:type :warn :msg msg}))
  (error [_ msg] (swap! messages conj {:type :error :msg msg})))
```

### 3. Update code
Replace:
```clojure
(println "[Isaac wants to share]:" content)
```

With:
```clojure
(ui/inform ui (str "[Isaac wants to share]: " content))
```

### 4. Inject UI
- Production: pass `(->ConsoleUI)`
- Tests: pass `(->MockUI (atom []))`

## Benefits
- Clean test output
- Testable UI interactions
- Can inspect what would have been printed
- Follows Dependency Inversion Principle

title: Add migration for user table

## Schema (suggested)
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  name VARCHAR(255),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
)
```

## Notes

- Coordinate with friend table (users may be friends)
